briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting critical risks in BaaS models.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a homegrown sovereign payment network, signalling a major shift away from Visa and Mastercard dominance.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking firm Credit Kudos signals Big Tech's growing appetite to embed financial data capabilities into its ecosystem.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Would you give an AI agent your credit card? Companies are betting so](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/ai-shopping-agentic-payments-9.7222389?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Companies are racing to integrate agentic AI into payment systems, betting that consumers will delegate purchasing decisions — and credit card access — to AI-powered shopping agents.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada officially labelled screen scraping as insecure and committed to an open banking framework, a foundational moment for Canada's financial data-sharing journey still reverberating today.

- **[DataBraid secures $1.9 million USD to solve insurance brokers' "portal hopping" problem](https://betakit.com/databraid-secures-1-9-million-usd-to-solve-insurance-brokers-portal-hopping-problem/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian insurtech startup DataBraid has raised $1.9M USD to streamline the fragmented workflow insurance brokers face when navigating multiple carrier portals.

- **[Canada Trade Surplus Widens to Largest Since Before Tariffs](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/canada-trade-surplus-widens-to-largest-since-before-tariffs)** — *Financial Post* — Canada's trade surplus hit its widest point since pre-tariff times in April, driven by record crude oil exports, with implications for business investment and financial market conditions.

- **[Why cybersecurity teams are struggling to keep up with AI adoption](https://betakit.com/why-cybersecurity-teams-are-struggling-to-keep-up-with-ai-adoption/)** — *BetaKit* — As AI tools proliferate across Canadian businesses, cybersecurity teams are finding it increasingly difficult to assess and manage the risks they introduce.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Public buying is a hidden growth engine. It's time for Canada to use it strategically](https://betakit.com/public-buying-is-a-hidden-growth-engine-its-time-for-canada-to-use-it-strategically/)** — *BetaKit* — This op-ed argues that Canadian governments should leverage public procurement as a deliberate tool to scale domestic tech companies — a strategy with direct implications for Atlantic fintech startups seeking early traction through government contracts.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is transforming credit card products with novel features and structures, a trend relevant to Atlantic Canadian credit unions and embedded finance players looking to differentiate their offerings.

- **[Is Canada's AI strategy a plan of action?](https://betakit.com/is-canadas-ai-strategy-a-plan-of-action/)** — *BetaKit* — BetaKit examines whether Canada's national AI strategy translates into meaningful action for the tech ecosystem — a critical question for Atlantic fintech founders building AI-enabled financial products and seeking federal support.
